title: [lora_l2.c:403-406 vs lichen_l2.c:1504] callback invocation order: mutex release before callback violates ordering
description: Cross-file analysis: In lora_l2.c rx_thread (lines 403-409), the callback is invoked AFTER releasing lora_mutex. The callback eventually reaches lichen_l2_input() which acquires rx_mutex. This is correct. However, there's a subtle cross-module lock ordering issue: if lichen_l2_input() ever needs to call back into lora_l2.c functions while holding rx_mutex (e.g., to send a response), and those functions acquire lora_mutex, we'd have: Thread A (RX): lora_mutex -> release -> rx_mutex. Thread B (TX): tx_mutex -> lora_l2_tx -> (no lora_mutex needed per current code). Currently safe because lora_l2_tx() doesn't acquire lora_mutex (line 864 shows tx_buf_mutex only), but the pattern is fragile. Document the cross-module lock independence as an invariant.
close_reason: Fixed: Added lock order invariant documentation in lora_l2.c rx_thread explaining mutex release before callback and cross-module lock independence requirement that lora_l2_tx must not acquire lora_mutex.
